items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Everyone in Lou Sang remembered the huge mulberry tree in front of Liu Bei's home.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: As a boy, Liu Bei would stand beneath it and look up, as if it belonged to some future not meant for the poor.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: But when he grew up, he still lived by weaving sandals and mats, making sure his mother at least had hot soup.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: When the recruitment notice went up, Liu Bei read every word and ended with a long sigh.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: It was not fear. He had ambition, but no money, no men, and no army.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Then a shout burst open behind him. This black-faced giant did not enter like a passerby. He entered like thunder.

frames.title: One Had Resolve, One Had Means

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Liu Bei spoke plainly: he wanted to fight the rebels, but he did not have the strength to begin.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Zhang Fei's eyes lit up at once. You have the will, he meant. I have the money. We can move now.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: The two men agreed at once and turned into the village tavern. For the first time that day, Liu Bei felt his sigh had been worth it.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Before the wine arrived, a new shadow fell across the doorway.
